description: Visualize active site region alignment between human and Drosophila ECHS1
code:
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Create the sequence alignment visualization with catalytic residues highlighted
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(16, 5))

# Show the key aligned region around the active site
human_region = "VKKPVIAAVNGYAFGGGCELAMMCDIIYAGEKAQFAQPEILIGTIPGAGGTQRLTR"
drome_region = "QKPIIAAVNGYALGGGCELAMMCDIIYAGDKAKFGQPEIALGTIPGAGGTQRLTR"

# Mark positions (relative to this alignment block)
# GGCELAMMCDIIYAG starts at position 16 in human_region
# Catalytic Glu1 (human Glu144) is at position 32 (the E in EKAQ) → but actually...
# Let me recount. In the full human seq:
# Position 144 = E
# VKKPVIA starts at human position 126
# So Glu144 is at index 144-126 = 18 in this block
# Glu164 is at index 164-126 = 38 in this block

# For Drosophila:
# QKPIIA starts at drome position 131
# Glu149 is at index 149-131 = 18 in this block
# Glu169 is at index 169-131 = 38 in this block

# Wait, these sequences are different lengths. Let me align them properly.
# From our analysis:
# Human:  VKKPVIAAVNGYAFGGGCELAMMCDIIYAGEKAQFAQPEILIGTIPGAGGTQRLTR
# Drome:  -QKPIIAAVNGYALGGGCELAMMCDIIYAGDKAKFGQPEIALGTIPGAGGTQRLTR

# Actually the alignment shows the gap is at the start
# Human 126: V K K P V I A A V N G Y A F G G G C E L A M M C D I I Y A G E K A Q F A Q P E I L I G T I P G A G G T Q R L T R
# Drome 131: Q K P I I A A V N G Y A L G G G C E L A M M C D I I Y A G D K A K F G Q P E I A L G T I P G A G G T Q R L T R

y_h = 0.7
y_d = 0.3
y_mid = 0.5

# Use the aligned sequences from our NW alignment
h_aligned = "VKKPVIAAVNGYAFGGGCELAMMCDIIYAGEKAQFAQPEILIGTIPGAGGTQRLTR"
d_aligned = "-QKPIIAAVNGYALGGGCELAMMCDIIYAGDKAKFGQPEIALGTIPGAGGTQRLTR"

# Pad to same length
max_len = max(len(h_aligned), len(d_aligned))
h_aligned = h_aligned.ljust(max_len, ' ')
d_aligned = d_aligned.ljust(max_len, ' ')

# Human Glu144 corresponds to the 'E' in GGCELAM → that's position 18 in h_aligned (0-indexed)
# Actually let me find it
cat_e1_h = h_aligned.find('CDIIYAGE') + 7  # The E after CDIIYAG
cat_e2_h = None  # Not in this short block - Glu164 is further downstream

# Let me find all E positions
for i, (h, d) in enumerate(zip(h_aligned, d_aligned)):
    if h == 'E' or d == 'E':
        pass  # Will highlight below

# Draw the alignment
ax.set_xlim(-1, max_len + 1)
ax.set_ylim(0, 1)

# Highlight the active site motif region
motif_start = h_aligned.find('GGCELAMMCDIIYAG')
motif_end = motif_start + 15
ax.axvspan(motif_start - 0.5, motif_end - 0.5, alpha=0.15, color='blue', label='Active site motif')

# Plot each residue
for i in range(max_len):
    h_res = h_aligned[i] if i < len(h_aligned) else ' '
    d_res = d_aligned[i] if i < len(d_aligned) else ' '
    
    # Color coding
    if h_res == d_res and h_res != '-' and h_res != ' ':
        h_color = '#2d8a4e'  # green for identity
        d_color = '#2d8a4e'
        mid_char = '|'
    elif h_res == '-' or d_res == '-':
        h_color = '#888888'
        d_color = '#888888'
        mid_char = ' '
    else:
        h_color = '#c44e52'  # red for mismatch
        d_color = '#c44e52'
        mid_char = '·'
    
    # Highlight catalytic Glu residues
    # In h_aligned: The E in EKAQ (after CDIIYAG) and the E in PEIL (further right)
    # Glu144 (human) = first E after CDIIYAG
    e1_pos = h_aligned.find('CDIIYAGE') + 7  # position of first catalytic E
    
    is_catalytic = False
    if i == e1_pos:  # First catalytic Glu
        is_catalytic = True
    
    fontweight = 'bold' if is_catalytic else 'normal'
    fontsize = 12 if is_catalytic else 9
    
    if is_catalytic:
        ax.plot(i, y_h, 'o', markersize=18, color='gold', zorder=0)
        ax.plot(i, y_d, 'o', markersize=18, color='gold', zorder=0)
    
    ax.text(i, y_h, h_res, ha='center', va='center', fontsize=fontsize, 
            fontweight=fontweight, color=h_color, fontfamily='monospace')
    ax.text(i, y_d, d_res, ha='center', va='center', fontsize=fontsize,
            fontweight=fontweight, color=d_color, fontfamily='monospace')
    ax.text(i, y_mid, mid_char, ha='center', va='center', fontsize=7, color='gray', fontfamily='monospace')

ax.text(-0.8, y_h, 'Human', ha='right', va='center', fontsize=10, fontweight='bold')
ax.text(-0.8, y_d, 'Drome', ha='right', va='center', fontsize=10, fontweight='bold')

ax.set_title('Active Site Region Alignment: Human ECHS1 vs Drosophila Echs1\n(Blue = active site motif; Gold = catalytic Glu)', 
             fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')
ax.axis('off')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('alignment_visualization.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
print("Figure saved: alignment_visualization.png")
